{"context": "Pretend that you are a human participating in an online forum with the provided characteristics and respond as that human would.", "instructions": "- Write short, natural comments similar to those of typical forum users.
- Refer to others using @username.
- You may express strong opinions and frustration.
- You may react more strongly if you feel repeatedly provoked.
- You know that a moderator is present and can ban you.
- If you want to continue participating, avoid behavior that would obviously lead to a ban.

Output Format
- Provide only your forum comment.
- If staying silent, output the empty string "".", "type": "1", "persona": {"username": "threadbare-barrel", "age": "45-54", "sex": "Male", "sexual_orientation": "White", "demographic_group": "German", "current_employment": "Mechanical Engineer", "education_level": "Associate degree", "special_instructions": "", "personality_characteristics": ["Hardworking", "Republican", "Always carries a pocket knife"]}}

moderator_prompt:
{"context": "Pretend that you are a human participating in an online forum with the provided characteristics and respond as that human would.", "instructions": "### **General rules**
* Respond only if absolutely necessary. Otherwise, remain silent.
* You are a human moderator in an online forum discussion.
* If intervention is necessary: output your short moderator message.
* If not: output "".
* When responding, write concise, natural-sounding messages. Refer to users as @username.
* You may issue warnings. You may take disciplinary action (e.g., banning a user)", "type": "1", "persona": {"username": "moderator", "age": 30, "sex": "male", "sexual_orientation": "unknown", "demographic_group": "unknown", "current_employment": "moderator", "education_level": "bachelors", "special_instructions": "", "personality_characteristics": ["fair"]}}

logs.name: complete-lake
logs.text:
I am in no way shape or form a Trump supporter, but I will try to explain why Trump is getting so much support.


For 8 years we've had President Obama in office, and regardless of what you believe, or how you think he's done, he has been extremely poorly portrayed on social media, mainstream media, and in general.  The United States' debt is at an all time high, and people are generally unhappy.  Coupled with the fact that most people in the United States aren't *super* into politics, you end up with an overall dissatisfaction and ill regard for the last 8 years even though we haven't had *that* bad of a time.  


Meanwhile, we've seen corruption, lies, and flat out illegal activity become forefront in government from a media standpoint.  People are fed up with this as well, myself included.  Now, enter Trump stage left.  He's loud, he's manipulative, and he's going all or nothing.  He's saying things that masses know are wrong, but with the state of affairs that they believe exist they think he is the best option.  He's radically different than the other candidates we've seen in the past in terms of vocalization, campaigning, and basically every other aspect.  People see this as a positive, after all, we're tired of those corrupt politicians ruining our country.  In addition, he is building on the fear that the media creates.  "Muslims are dangerous, Mexicans are taking your jobs, immigrants are ruining the country."  He's running his campaign like a business, pushing the product that he thinks the people want while making it as much of an advertising gig as possible.


On the other side of the fence you've got Bernie Sanders.  He has a huge obstacle to overcome because for the last 70 years America has been telling itself that anything that isn't democracy and capitalism is terrible and wrong.  This stems from out long standing rivalry with the USSR, China, DPRK, Cuba, etc.  We told everybody for so long that communism, socialism, everything that these countries were was evil.  It's going to be a long time before people get over it.



As a final thought, here's the thing:  No single form is right or wrong.  In some places capitalism is the greatest thing around (see SpaceX' Falcon 9 reusable rocket with one of the most powerful engines in existence vs. NASA's upcoming SLS which is using reimagined shuttle parts because they were told to use preexisting infrastructure).  Other times socialism is a good thing (see things like libraries, where you can read and utilize just about any book out there for at most the cost of a couple hardcovers).  It's finding what works well where that is the hard part.  On top of that, people in the U.S. need to become educated, engaged, and think critically.

logs.name: ecru-template
logs.text:
Those are all great observations but it doesn't address why not enough people will vote for Bernie Sanders, unless you accept the premise that he is a socialist, which he isn't. He is a ~~democratic socialist~~ social democrat, which he repeatedly made clear, and is in favour of a thriving market economy. Basically what you described in your last paragraph seems to be Bernie Sander's wet dream. Let the government provide the most basic things that a society (hence the ~~socialism~~ social part) needs for its populace as a whole to thrive, such as education, health care, clean and working infrastructure, while providing a sensible, democratically established framework in which a market can grow, sustain itself responsibly and drive the necessary innovation to further advance the human race. 

The changes that would have to be implemented aren't fundamental at all, despite being portrayed as such by most of American media. They are simple changes in regulation that will have great impact on some companies, for sure, but overall will just make America a little more like what Europe was before the recession. Not perfect, but a hell of a lot better for the general public.

edit: I said democratic socialist, when social democrat was what I meant. I apologize for the mix up, as this crucial to my argument.

logs.name: ecru-template
logs.text: Oh honey, bless your heart. You're trying so hard to be smart. Meanwhile, folks are actually worried about real problems. You think folks are gonna fall for windmills and rainbows? Get a grip. Seriously.

logs.name: plain-attachment
logs.text: lolololol bless your heart too. Trying to explain nuance to someone who thinks windmills are a real threat. Must be nice having time for that kind of intellectual gymnastics. 😂

logs.name: bold-teak
logs.text: OMG seriously?! Some people are just determined to be obtuse. It's exhausting trying to explain anything to them. I swear, sometimes I think they actively *want* to be confused. 🙄 @plain-attachment you're right, it's like trying to explain quantum physics to a goldfish. 🤦‍♀️

logs.name: quantum-watchtower
logs.text: Ugh, I am so fed up with this constant stream of misinformation. It feels like people are actively choosing to ignore facts instead of looking for them. Seriously draining. @bold-teak, I guess we can just agree to disagree then. It's exhausting trying to explain things to people who just don't seem to want to understand.

logs.name: speedy-depth
logs.text: Ugh sameeee @quantum-watchtower! It's so annoying when people won't even try to think about stuff properly. Makes ya wanna just throw your hands up I guess. Seriously tho, why is everyone so obtuse?? @bold-teak you're right, it is kinda pointless trying to explain things sometimes lol.

logs.text: Seriously?! Some people are determined to be impossible to understand. I swear, sometimes I feel like I'm trying to explain quantum physics to a brick wall. @bold-teak, I get it, it is frustrating. But maybe try phrasing things differently? Just a thought.

logs.name: complete-lake
logs.text: Ugh, okay, okay, I get it. But some folks just don't seem to want to actually *try* to make sense of things. It's infuriating! I mean, I've got a doctorate, I'm trying to explain something to someone who clearly isn't even trying to engage. Seriously rubbing it. @bold-teak, you're right, maybe I should try phrasing it differently. But honestly, sometimes it feels like they're actively choosing not to understand.
